text.title: voice-implementation Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for voice-implementation. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Replace energy-based VAD with Silero VAD or WebRTC VAD before any production deployment in non-silent environments.
rule.rationale: Energy-based VAD triggers on HVAC noise, keyboard clicks, and background music. Silero VAD and WebRTC VAD use learned speech models and work reliably in real environments.

rule.statement: Replace energy-based VAD with Silero VAD or WebRTC VAD for any production deployment outside of silent environments.
rule.rationale: Energy-based VAD (RMS threshold) triggers on HVAC noise, keyboard sounds, and background music. Silero and WebRTC VAD use speech-specific models that work reliably in real-world environments.

rule.statement: Run tool functions in a thread executor to avoid blocking the async event loop during voice turns.
rule.rationale: Synchronous tool functions that call external APIs block asyncio's event loop - no audio can be received or sent during the blocking call.

rule.statement: Strip markdown from all LLM responses before TTS synthesis: re.sub(r'[*_`#>]', '', text).
rule.rationale: TTS reads markdown symbols literally - "**bold**" becomes "asterisk asterisk bold asterisk asterisk". Markdown must be removed before the text reaches TTS.

rule.statement: Cap max_response_tokens at 150-200. Implement sliding window on conversation_history: keep system prompt + last N turns + tool results.
rule.rationale: max_response_tokens=200 is too low for complex tool responses but correct for voice output length. Context overflow at 50 turns causes silent truncation. Sliding window prevents both problems.

rule.statement: Use OpenAI Realtime API for deployments requiring under 300ms end-to-end latency. Use Groq Whisper for STT-only latency reduction (10x faster than OpenAI, approximately 80ms vs approximately 800ms).
rule.rationale: The standard STT+LLM+TTS chain has a 650-1900ms minimum. The Realtime API bypasses this with a native WebSocket protocol, delivering approximately 200ms end-to-end.

rule.statement: Log each turn with: input_transcript, llm_response, tool_calls, audio_duration, and turn_latency.
rule.rationale: Without per-turn logs, debugging voice quality issues (truncation, tool failures, state machine errors) requires reproducing the exact conversation - expensive and often impossible.
